items.issue: Response table claims 'not JSON' is rejected with 400, but the controller's consumes=application/json means a non-JSON Content-Type is rejected with 415 Unsupported Media Type; 400 only covers a missing or malformed body sent as JSON
items.fix: Split the row: 400 for missing/malformed JSON body, 415 for a non-JSON Content-Type (or reword the 400 condition to 'body is missing or malformed JSON' and add a 415 row)

items.issue: The 'Start just infrastructure' flow implies running the service natively against the emulator, but the local profile's placeholder connection string (application-local.yml) points at local.servicebus.windows.net without UseDevelopmentEmulator=true, so a native 'mvn spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=local' will fail every send with 502 instead of reaching the emulator
items.fix: Document the export needed for native-against-emulator runs (AZURE_SERVICE_BUS_CONNECTION_STRING="Endpoint=sb://localhost;SharedAccessKeyName=RootManageSharedAccessKey;SharedAccessKey=SAS_KEY_VALUE;UseDevelopmentEmulator=true;") or state explicitly that the local profile defaults do not connect to the emulator

items.issue: servicebus-emulator uses the unpinned :latest tag while the sibling localstack service is pinned to 3.0.2 — an upstream emulator release can silently break every developer's local stack
items.fix: Pin the image to a specific version tag (e.g. mcr.microsoft.com/azure-messaging/servicebus-emulator:1.1.2), matching the pinned-localstack pattern in this file

items.issue: Gateway depends_on servicebus-emulator with condition: service_started and the emulator has no healthcheck, so the app can start before the emulator accepts AMQP connections (mssql gets service_healthy; the emulator does not)
items.fix: Add a healthcheck to servicebus-emulator (e.g. TCP probe on 5672 or the emulator's documented readiness check) and change the gateway dependency to condition: service_healthy

items.issue: azure-messaging-servicebus version 7.17.11 is pinned directly on the artifact instead of importing the Azure SDK BOM (com.azure:azure-sdk-bom) in dependencyManagement; future Azure artifacts (e.g. azure-identity when SAS auth is swapped per the ticket) can drift out of sync
items.fix: Import com.azure:azure-sdk-bom in dependencyManagement (alongside the existing AWS BOM) and drop the explicit version from azure-messaging-servicebus

items.issue: Per-artifact <version>${testcontainers.version}</version> on org.testcontainers:azure and :mssqlserver is redundant — spring-boot-starter-parent already imports the testcontainers BOM and honours the testcontainers.version property override set at line 27
items.fix: Keep the <testcontainers.version>1.21.4</testcontainers.version> property override and remove the explicit <version> elements from both testcontainers dependencies so they resolve via the Boot-managed testcontainers BOM

items.issue: ServiceBusClientBuilder uses the default AMQP-over-TCP transport (port 5671), which bypasses the CDP egress proxy ProxyConfig sets via ProxySelector/system properties — the Azure SDK only honours a proxy with AMQP_WEB_SOCKETS, so the sender works locally and against the emulator ITs but cannot connect to ASB from a deployed CDP environment (breaks the send-to-ASB AC there)
items.fix: Add .transportType(AmqpTransportType.AMQP_WEB_SOCKETS) to the builder so AMQP is tunnelled over 443 and the default ProxySelector from ProxyConfig is honoured (or pass explicit ProxyOptions built from cdp.proxyUrl)

items.issue: Forward-looking migration note ('Once auth is confirmed, migrate to spring-cloud-azure-starter-servicebus and remove this class') is an in-file TODO that will rot; workspace convention keeps migration intent in tickets/git history, not code comments
items.fix: Keep the two rationale lines explaining the manual wiring; drop the migration-instruction line and track the starter migration in the SDA-review follow-up ticket

items.issue: Record auto-generates toString() that includes the full SAS connectionString, so any future logging of the config bean (or a framework error report rendering it) would leak the SharedAccessKey
items.fix: Override toString() in the record to redact connectionString (e.g. print queue only, mask the connection string)

items.issue: publishEvent has no @Timed annotation even though MetricsConfig already registers TimedAspect, so the gateway's only business endpoint emits no per-endpoint Micrometer metric
items.fix: Add @Timed("events.publish") to publishEvent, matching the per-endpoint metric convention

items.issue: Error responses are ad-hoc Map.of("error", ...) bodies instead of RFC 7807 ProblemDetail with a /problems/... type URI and traceId — the repo's RequestTracingFilter puts traceId in MDC but it is never surfaced in error responses, and this PR establishes the error contract for the whole new service
items.fix: Return ProblemDetail.forStatusAndDetail(...) from both handlers, set problem.setType(URI.create("/problems/...")) and problem.setProperty("traceId", MDC.get("traceId")) per the documented GlobalExceptionHandler pattern

items.issue: No catch-all @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) — unexpected exceptions fall through to Spring Boot's default error attributes, giving a different error body shape from the {"error": ...} responses defined here
items.fix: Add a handleException(Exception ex) handler that logs at error level and returns 500 with the same body shape ("An unexpected error occurred"), matching the documented GlobalExceptionHandler pattern

items.issue: post_returnsAccepted_forValidJson verifies publish(any(JsonNode.class)) but never asserts the forwarded content equals the posted body — AC requires the endpoint to send 'that content' to ASB, and a controller forwarding a wrong/empty node would still pass
items.fix: Capture the argument (ArgumentCaptor<JsonNode> or eq(objectMapper.readTree(...))) and assert the published JsonNode equals the posted JSON

items.issue: Rejection tests (missing body, malformed JSON, unsupported media type) assert only the 4xx status — none verify queueMessageSender was never invoked, leaving the AC clause 'is rejected with a 4xx response and is not sent to ASB' untested
items.fix: Add verifyNoInteractions(queueMessageSender) (or verify(queueMessageSender, never()).publish(any())) to each rejection test

items.issue: Tests omit the // Given / // When / // Then comment blocks that the unit-testing guide mandates for all tests (short tests may combine as // When & Then)
items.fix: Add // Given / // When & Then comment blocks to each test method

items.issue: No test asserts the ServiceBusMessage body equals the serialised input JSON — the core AC behaviour (posted content forwarded to ASB) is unpinned; publish could send an empty/wrong payload and all tests stay green
items.fix: In publish_sendsMessageToServiceBus, capture the message and assert sent.getBody().toString() equals the input JSON (e.g. {"key":"value"}) instead of verify(...).sendMessage(any(...))

items.issue: Tests omit the explicit // Given, // When, // Then comment blocks required by the unit-testing guide
items.fix: Add // Given / // When / // Then comment blocks to each test (// When & Then may be combined for the exception test)

items.issue: Test names (publish_sendsMessageToServiceBus, publish_setsContentTypeAndMessageId, publish_throwsGatewayException_whenSendFails) drop the 'should' form required by the {subject}_{shouldDoWhat}[_{whenContext}] convention
items.fix: Rename to publish_shouldSendMessageToServiceBus, publish_shouldSetContentTypeAndMessageId, publish_shouldThrowGatewayException_whenSendFails

items.issue: Test method names use {subject}_{doesWhat} (handleUnreadableBody_returns400WithErrorMessage, handleGatewayException_returns502WithErrorMessage) instead of the project convention {subject}_{shouldDoWhat}
items.fix: Rename to handleUnreadableBody_shouldReturn400WithErrorMessage and handleGatewayException_shouldReturn502WithErrorMessage

items.issue: Tests lack the explicit // Given, // When, // Then comment blocks the unit-testing guide mandates for all tests
items.fix: Add // Given / // When / // Then (or combined // When & Then) comment blocks to both test methods

items.issue: AC says a missing/malformed body 'is rejected with a 4xx response and is not sent to ASB' — both 400 tests assert only the status/body, never that nothing reached Service Bus, and no other test (unit or IT) pins that clause
items.fix: In post_returnsBadRequest_forMissingBody and post_returnsBadRequest_forMalformedJson add Mockito.verifyNoInteractions(senderClient) (or assert the queue is empty) after the status assertion

items.issue: receiveMessage() builds the receiver in the default PEEK_LOCK mode and never settles the received message, so after the lock expires the message returns to the shared static queue and can be redelivered into later tests/classes as the suite grows
items.fix: Add .receiveMode(ServiceBusReceiveMode.RECEIVE_AND_DELETE) to the receiver builder (or call receiver.complete(received) before returning)

items.issue: Happy-path test asserts the received body merely contains 'NotificationSubmitted' — a substring check would still pass if the gateway wrapped or mangled the payload, so it doesn't pin the AC's 'sends that content' forwarding fidelity
items.fix: Assert the exact forwarded body (isEqualTo the posted JSON) and the message content type (received.getRawAmqpMessage()/getContentType() == application/json) which QueueMessageSender sets

items.issue: No // Given / When / Then comment blocks in any test — the integration-testing guide says the comment blocks must be present even when When/Then are combined in a fluent chain
items.fix: Add // Given, // When, // Then comments to each test method

items.issue: Test names use post_returnsX_whenY instead of the documented {subject}_{shouldDoWhat}_{whenContext} pattern (e.g. post_shouldReturnAccepted_whenValidJson) — same deviation across all four tests
items.fix: Rename tests to the should-form, e.g. post_shouldReturnAccepted_andMessageLandsInQueue, post_shouldReturnBadGateway_whenSendFails

items.issue: Service Bus emulator image uses floating 'latest' tag (and mssql uses floating '2022-latest'), so an upstream emulator release can break all ITs with no code change; project convention pins container tags (mongo:7.0, mock-oauth2-server:2.1.10) and Testcontainers docs pin servicebus-emulator:1.0.1
items.fix: Pin both images to specific tags, e.g. mcr.microsoft.com/azure-messaging/servicebus-emulator:1.1.2 and mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-CU14-ubuntu-22.04

items.issue: Renamed test method customSslContext_isTlsAndWiredBySpring drops the project's subject_shouldDoWhat naming convention (old name customSslContext_shouldBeConfigured conformed)
items.fix: Rename to customSslContext_shouldBeTlsAndWiredBySpring to match the subject_shouldDoWhat convention
